1	# CsvReportGenerator.ps1
2	# Functions for reading employee CSV data, computing aggregates, and generating reports.
3	# Designed with TDD — each function was created to satisfy a specific set of Pester tests.
4	
5	<#
6	.SYNOPSIS
7	    Imports a CSV file of employee records and returns only active employees.
8	.DESCRIPTION
9	    Reads the CSV, validates required columns exist, filters to active status,
10	    and casts salary to [double] and hire_date to [datetime].
11	.PARAMETER Path
12	    Path to the CSV file.
13	#>
14	function Import-EmployeeCsv {
15	    param(
16	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
17	        [string]$Path
18	    )
19	
20	    # Validate file exists
21	    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $Path)) {
22	        throw "File '$Path' does not exist."
23	    }
24	
25	    # Read raw CSV
26	    $raw = Import-Csv -Path $Path
27	
28	    # Validate required columns
29	    $requiredColumns = @('name', 'department', 'salary', 'hire_date', 'status')
30	    if ($raw.Count -gt 0) {
31	        $actualColumns = $raw[0].PSObject.Properties.Name
32	        $missing = $requiredColumns | Where-Object { $_ -notin $actualColumns }
33	        if ($missing.Count -gt 0) {
34	            throw "Missing required columns: $($missing -join ', ')"
35	        }
36	    }
37	    else {
38	        # Check headers even when there are no data rows
39	        $headerLine = Get-Content -Path $Path -TotalCount 1
40	        if ($headerLine) {
41	            $headers = $headerLine.Split(',') | ForEach-Object { $_.Trim() }
42	            $missing = $requiredColumns | Where-Object { $_ -notin $headers }
43	            if ($missing.Count -gt 0) {
44	                throw "Missing required columns: $($missing -join ', ')"
45	            }
46	        }
47	        # No data rows — return empty array
48	        return @()
49	    }
50	
51	    # Filter to active employees and cast types
52	    $active = $raw | Where-Object { $_.status -eq 'active' } | ForEach-Object {
53	        [PSCustomObject]@{
54	            name       = $_.name
55	            department = $_.department
56	            salary     = [double]$_.salary
57	            hire_date  = [datetime]$_.hire_date
58	            status     = $_.status
59	        }
60	    }
61	
62	    # Ensure we always return an array (even for 0 or 1 results)
63	    return @($active)
64	}
65	
66	<#
67	.SYNOPSIS
68	    Computes average salary and headcount grouped by department.
69	.PARAMETER Employees
70	    Array of employee objects (already filtered to active).
71	.OUTPUTS
72	    Array of objects with Department, AverageSalary, and Headcount properties.
73	#>
74	function Get-DepartmentAggregates {
75	    param(
76	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
77	        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
78	        [array]$Employees
79	    )
80	
81	    if ($Employees.Count -eq 0) {
82	        return @()
83	    }
84	
85	    $groups = $Employees | Group-Object -Property department | Sort-Object -Property Name
86	
87	    $results = $groups | ForEach-Object {
88	        $salaries = $_.Group | ForEach-Object { $_.salary }
89	        $avg = ($salaries | Measure-Object -Average).Average
90	
91	        [PSCustomObject]@{
92	            Department    = $_.Name
93	            AverageSalary = [math]::Round($avg, 2)
94	            Headcount     = $_.Count
95	        }
96	    }
97	
98	    return @($results)
99	}
100	
101	<#
102	.SYNOPSIS
103	    Computes overall statistics across all employees.
104	.PARAMETER Employees
105	    Array of employee objects.
106	.OUTPUTS
107	    Object with TotalHeadcount, TotalSalary, AverageSalary, MinSalary, MaxSalary, DepartmentCount.
108	#>
109	function Get-OverallStatistics {
110	    param(
111	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
112	        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
113	        [array]$Employees
114	    )
115	
116	    if ($Employees.Count -eq 0) {
117	        return [PSCustomObject]@{
118	            TotalHeadcount  = 0
119	            TotalSalary     = 0
120	            AverageSalary   = 0
121	            MinSalary       = 0
122	            MaxSalary       = 0
123	            DepartmentCount = 0
124	        }
125	    }
126	
127	    $measure = $Employees | ForEach-Object { $_.salary } | Measure-Object -Sum -Average -Minimum -Maximum
128	    $deptCount = ($Employees | Select-Object -Property department -Unique).Count
129	
130	    return [PSCustomObject]@{
131	        TotalHeadcount  = $Employees.Count
132	        TotalSalary     = $measure.Sum
133	        AverageSalary   = [math]::Round($measure.Average, 2)
134	        MinSalary       = $measure.Minimum
135	        MaxSalary       = $measure.Maximum
136	        DepartmentCount = $deptCount
137	    }
138	}
139	
140	<#
141	.SYNOPSIS
142	    Generates a formatted summary report string from employee data.
143	.PARAMETER Employees
144	    Array of employee objects.
145	.OUTPUTS
146	    A multi-line string containing the formatted report.
147	#>
148	function New-SummaryReport {
149	    param(
150	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
151	        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
152	        [array]$Employees
153	    )
154	
155	    $aggregates = Get-DepartmentAggregates -Employees $Employees
156	    $stats      = Get-OverallStatistics -Employees $Employees
157	    $separator  = "=" * 60
158	
159	    $sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()
160	    [void]$sb.AppendLine($separator)
161	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("           Employee Summary Report")
162	    [void]$sb.AppendLine($separator)
163	    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
164	
165	    # Department breakdown section
166	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Department Breakdown")
167	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  " + ("-" * 50))
168	    [void]$sb.AppendLine(("  {0,-20} {1,10} {2,15}" -f "Department", "Headcount", "Avg Salary"))
169	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  " + ("-" * 50))
170	
171	    foreach ($dept in $aggregates) {
172	        $formattedSalary = "{0:N0}" -f $dept.AverageSalary
173	        [void]$sb.AppendLine(("  {0,-20} {1,10} {2,15}" -f $dept.Department, $dept.Headcount, $formattedSalary))
174	    }
175	
176	    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
177	
178	    # Overall statistics section
179	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Overall Statistics")
180	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  " + ("-" * 50))
181	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Total Headcount:    $($stats.TotalHeadcount)")
182	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Total Salary:       $("{0:N0}" -f $stats.TotalSalary)")
183	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Average Salary:     $("{0:N0}" -f $stats.AverageSalary)")
184	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Min Salary:         $("{0:N0}" -f $stats.MinSalary)")
185	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Max Salary:         $("{0:N0}" -f $stats.MaxSalary)")
186	    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Departments:        $($stats.DepartmentCount)")
187	    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
188	    [void]$sb.AppendLine($separator)
189	
190	    return $sb.ToString()
191	}
192	
193	<#
194	.SYNOPSIS
195	    Generates and writes the summary report to a text file.
196	.PARAMETER Employees
197	    Array of employee objects.
198	.PARAMETER OutputPath
199	    Path to the output text file.
200	#>
201	function Export-SummaryReport {
202	    param(
203	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
204	        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
205	        [array]$Employees,
206	
207	        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
208	        [string]$OutputPath
209	    )
210	
211	    $report = New-SummaryReport -Employees $Employees
212	
213	    # Ensure the output directory exists
214	    $dir = Split-Path -Path $OutputPath -Parent
215	    if ($dir -and -not (Test-Path -Path $dir)) {
216	        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $dir -Force | Out-Null
217	    }
218	
219	    $report | Set-Content -Path $OutputPath -Encoding UTF8
220	    Write-Host "Report written to: $OutputPath"
221	}
222

file.content:
# CsvReportGenerator.ps1
# Functions for reading employee CSV data, computing aggregates, and generating reports.
# Designed with TDD — each function was created to satisfy a specific set of Pester tests.

<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Imports a CSV file of employee records and returns only active employees.
.DESCRIPTION
    Reads the CSV, validates required columns exist, filters to active status,
    and casts salary to [double] and hire_date to [datetime].
.PARAMETER Path
    Path to the CSV file.
#>
function Import-EmployeeCsv {
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$Path
    )

    # Validate file exists
    if (-not (Test-Path -Path $Path)) {
        throw "File '$Path' does not exist."
    }

    # Read raw CSV
    $raw = Import-Csv -Path $Path

    # Validate required columns
    $requiredColumns = @('name', 'department', 'salary', 'hire_date', 'status')
    if ($raw.Count -gt 0) {
        $actualColumns = $raw[0].PSObject.Properties.Name
        $missing = $requiredColumns | Where-Object { $_ -notin $actualColumns }
        if ($missing.Count -gt 0) {
            throw "Missing required columns: $($missing -join ', ')"
        }
    }
    else {
        # Check headers even when there are no data rows
        $headerLine = Get-Content -Path $Path -TotalCount 1
        if ($headerLine) {
            $headers = $headerLine.Split(',') | ForEach-Object { $_.Trim() }
            $missing = $requiredColumns | Where-Object { $_ -notin $headers }
            if ($missing.Count -gt 0) {
                throw "Missing required columns: $($missing -join ', ')"
            }
        }
        # No data rows — return empty array
        return @()
    }

    # Filter to active employees and cast types
    $active = $raw | Where-Object { $_.status -eq 'active' } | ForEach-Object {
        [PSCustomObject]@{
            name       = $_.name
            department = $_.department
            salary     = [double]$_.salary
            hire_date  = [datetime]$_.hire_date
            status     = $_.status
        }
    }

    # Ensure we always return an array (even for 0 or 1 results)
    return @($active)
}

<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Computes average salary and headcount grouped by department.
.PARAMETER Employees
    Array of employee objects (already filtered to active).
.OUTPUTS
    Array of objects with Department, AverageSalary, and Headcount properties.
#>
function Get-DepartmentAggregates {
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
        [array]$Employees
    )

    if ($Employees.Count -eq 0) {
        return @()
    }

    $groups = $Employees | Group-Object -Property department | Sort-Object -Property Name

    $results = $groups | ForEach-Object {
        $salaries = $_.Group | ForEach-Object { $_.salary }
        $avg = ($salaries | Measure-Object -Average).Average

        [PSCustomObject]@{
            Department    = $_.Name
            AverageSalary = [math]::Round($avg, 2)
            Headcount     = $_.Count
        }
    }

    return @($results)
}

<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Computes overall statistics across all employees.
.PARAMETER Employees
    Array of employee objects.
.OUTPUTS
    Object with TotalHeadcount, TotalSalary, AverageSalary, MinSalary, MaxSalary, DepartmentCount.
#>
function Get-OverallStatistics {
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
        [array]$Employees
    )

    if ($Employees.Count -eq 0) {
        return [PSCustomObject]@{
            TotalHeadcount  = 0
            TotalSalary     = 0
            AverageSalary   = 0
            MinSalary       = 0
            MaxSalary       = 0
            DepartmentCount = 0
        }
    }

    $measure = $Employees | ForEach-Object { $_.salary } | Measure-Object -Sum -Average -Minimum -Maximum
    $deptCount = ($Employees | Select-Object -Property department -Unique).Count

    return [PSCustomObject]@{
        TotalHeadcount  = $Employees.Count
        TotalSalary     = $measure.Sum
        AverageSalary   = [math]::Round($measure.Average, 2)
        MinSalary       = $measure.Minimum
        MaxSalary       = $measure.Maximum
        DepartmentCount = $deptCount
    }
}

<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Generates a formatted summary report string from employee data.
.PARAMETER Employees
    Array of employee objects.
.OUTPUTS
    A multi-line string containing the formatted report.
#>
function New-SummaryReport {
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
        [array]$Employees
    )

    $aggregates = Get-DepartmentAggregates -Employees $Employees
    $stats      = Get-OverallStatistics -Employees $Employees
    $separator  = "=" * 60

    $sb = [System.Text.StringBuilder]::new()
    [void]$sb.AppendLine($separator)
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("           Employee Summary Report")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine($separator)
    [void]$sb.AppendLine()

    # Department breakdown section
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Department Breakdown")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  " + ("-" * 50))
    [void]$sb.AppendLine(("  {0,-20} {1,10} {2,15}" -f "Department", "Headcount", "Avg Salary"))
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  " + ("-" * 50))

    foreach ($dept in $aggregates) {
        $formattedSalary = "{0:N0}" -f $dept.AverageSalary
        [void]$sb.AppendLine(("  {0,-20} {1,10} {2,15}" -f $dept.Department, $dept.Headcount, $formattedSalary))
    }

    [void]$sb.AppendLine()

    # Overall statistics section
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Overall Statistics")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  " + ("-" * 50))
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Total Headcount:    $($stats.TotalHeadcount)")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Total Salary:       $("{0:N0}" -f $stats.TotalSalary)")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Average Salary:     $("{0:N0}" -f $stats.AverageSalary)")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Min Salary:         $("{0:N0}" -f $stats.MinSalary)")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Max Salary:         $("{0:N0}" -f $stats.MaxSalary)")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine("  Departments:        $($stats.DepartmentCount)")
    [void]$sb.AppendLine()
    [void]$sb.AppendLine($separator)

    return $sb.ToString()
}

<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Generates and writes the summary report to a text file.
.PARAMETER Employees
    Array of employee objects.
.PARAMETER OutputPath
    Path to the output text file.
#>
function Export-SummaryReport {
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [AllowEmptyCollection()]
        [array]$Employees,

        [Parameter(Mandatory)]
        [string]$OutputPath
    )

    $report = New-SummaryReport -Employees $Employees

    # Ensure the output directory exists
    $dir = Split-Path -Path $OutputPath -Parent
    if ($dir -and -not (Test-Path -Path $dir)) {
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $dir -Force | Out-Null
    }

    $report | Set-Content -Path $OutputPath -Encoding UTF8
    Write-Host "Report written to: $OutputPath"
}
